mnemonics: SICK VM: Shared Images Can Kill Virtual Machines. Think of a shared image as a 'sick' VM that can infect every new instance it creates.
keyPoints: A shared golden image containing a vulnerability or malware will propagate that security flaw to every new compute instance deployed from it.
The management OS (hypervisor) has elevated access to all guest VMs. If compromised, an attacker can potentially control all VMs on the host.
Tenants in a shared infrastructure model have no control over the security of the underlying management OS, relying entirely on the cloud provider.
commonMistakes: Assuming a cloud provider's pre-configured image is automatically secure and not performing your own vulnerability assessment.
Forgetting that deprovisioning an instance does not automatically delete the shared image that was used to create it, leaving a potential risk active.
Underestimating the 'blast radius' of a compromised management OS, believing tenant VMs are fully isolated.
